---
title: CLI & SDK
description: The open-managed-agents Node CLI and the @open-managed-agents/api-types package — your two ways to talk to the API without curl.
---

import { Tabs, TabItem, Aside, LinkCard } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

openma ships two developer-facing packages:

- **`open-managed-agents`** — a Node CLI for scripting against the API.
- **`@open-managed-agents/api-types`** — pure-TypeScript DTOs of the wire format. Zero workspace dependencies; safe to import from a CLI, a Worker, the Console, or your own integrations.

Both live in this repo (`packages/cli`, `packages/api-types`).

## CLI

### Install

<Tabs syncKey="pkg-mgr">
  <TabItem label="npm">
    ```bash
    npm i -g open-managed-agents
    ```
  </TabItem>
  <TabItem label="pnpm">
    ```bash
    pnpm add -g open-managed-agents
    ```
  </TabItem>
  <TabItem label="bun">
    ```bash
    bun add -g open-managed-agents
    ```
  </TabItem>
</Tabs>

### Configure

The CLI reads two env vars:

```bash
export OMA_BASE_URL="https://app.openma.dev"
export OMA_API_KEY="oma_..."
```

For self-hosters, point `OMA_BASE_URL` at your own deployment.

### Common commands

```bash
# List agents
oma agents list

# Create an agent from a JSON file
oma agents create -f ./my-agent.json

# Start a session
oma sessions create --agent agent_abc123 --env env_abc123 --title "research"

# Chat — post a turn AND stream the reply token-by-token to your terminal
oma sessions chat sess_xyz "Summarize the openma docs."

# Fire-and-forget message (no streaming back; useful when something else is tailing)
oma sessions message sess_xyz "Now also include the integrations."

# Tail a session's full event stream (SSE under the hood)
oma sessions tail sess_xyz

# Replay a session's full event log
oma sessions logs sess_xyz

# Show your usage
oma usage
```

Run `oma --help` or `oma <command> --help` for the full surface.

<Aside type="tip" title="Pipe-friendly">
  CLI subcommands accept `--json` and emit machine-readable output. Pipe into `jq` for ad-hoc analysis.
</Aside>

## SDK (api-types)

`@open-managed-agents/api-types` is a tiny package: just the TypeScript types for every request and response shape. No HTTP client, no runtime — bring your own `fetch`.

### Install

<Tabs syncKey="pkg-mgr">
  <TabItem label="npm">
    ```bash
    npm i @open-managed-agents/api-types
    ```
  </TabItem>
  <TabItem label="pnpm">
    ```bash
    pnpm add @open-managed-agents/api-types
    ```
  </TabItem>
  <TabItem label="bun">
    ```bash
    bun add @open-managed-agents/api-types
    ```
  </TabItem>
</Tabs>

### Use

```ts
import type {
  AgentConfig,
  CreateAgentRequest,
  CreateAgentResponse,
  SessionEvent,
  SessionMeta,
  ContentBlock,
} from '@open-managed-agents/api-types';

const baseUrl = process.env.OMA_BASE_URL!;
const apiKey = process.env.OMA_API_KEY!;

async function createAgent(req: CreateAgentRequest): Promise<CreateAgentResponse> {
  const res = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/v1/agents`, {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: {
      'x-api-key': apiKey,
      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    },
    body: JSON.stringify(req),
  });
  if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${res.status}`);
  return res.json();
}
```

### Why no client library?

Because everything is HTTP and SSE — well-supported in every runtime (Node, Bun, Deno, browsers, Workers). Wrapping it would just hide what's actually happening. The DTOs give you autocomplete and compile-time safety; the wire format is documented in [REST API](/build/api/) and [Reference → API Endpoints](/reference/api/).

If you want a typed wrapper, the CLI source (`packages/cli/src/`) is a small reference implementation you can lift.

## Talking to the SSE stream

Two ways to drive a turn:

**(a) Chatbot one-shot**: post the user message *and* receive the response stream in a single request. Auto-closes when the turn ends.

```ts
import type { SessionEvent } from '@open-managed-agents/api-types';

const res = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/v1/sessions/${sessionId}/messages`, {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: { 'x-api-key': apiKey, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
  body: JSON.stringify({ content: 'Hello!' }),
});

for await (const event of parseSSE<SessionEvent>(res)) {
  switch (event.type) {
    case 'agent.message_chunk':
      // Live token deltas — render incrementally; replace at the end
      // when the canonical agent.message lands with the same message_id.
      process.stdout.write(event.delta);
      break;
    case 'agent.thinking_chunk':
      // Live extended-thinking deltas. Same idea, correlate by thinking_id.
      process.stderr.write(event.delta);
      break;
    case 'agent.tool_use_input_chunk':
      // Live tool-input JSON deltas. Partial JSON; render in a code block.
      // Drop the in-flight render when agent.tool_use lands with the same id.
      break;
    case 'agent.message':
      // Canonical text message — same message_id as the chunks above.
      // Drop your in-flight buffer; this content is the source of truth.
      break;
    case 'session.status_idle':
      return; // turn done; the server will also close the stream
  }
}

async function* parseSSE<T>(res: Response): AsyncIterable<T> {
  const reader = res.body!.getReader();
  const dec = new TextDecoder();
  let buf = '';
  while (true) {
    const { done, value } = await reader.read();
    if (done) return;
    buf += dec.decode(value, { stream: true });
    let i;
    while ((i = buf.indexOf('\n\n')) !== -1) {
      const chunk = buf.slice(0, i); buf = buf.slice(i + 2);
      const line = chunk.split('\n').find(l => l.startsWith('data: '));
      if (line) yield JSON.parse(line.slice(6)) as T;
    }
  }
}
```

**(b) Tail a long-lived session**: same `parseSSE` helper, but use `GET /v1/sessions/:id/events/stream` instead — never closes, replays history on connect, sees every future event.

```ts
const res = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/v1/sessions/${sessionId}/events/stream`, {
  headers: { 'x-api-key': apiKey, 'Accept': 'text/event-stream' },
});
for await (const event of parseSSE<SessionEvent>(res)) {
  // ...same switch as above
}
```

The CLI's `oma sessions chat` and `oma sessions tail` are both small wrappers around this pattern.
